name: Anti-Sudoku
input:
The first line of the input contains one integer $t$ ($1 \le t \le 10^4$) — the number of test cases. Then $t$ test cases follow.
Each test case consists of $9$ lines; each line consists of $9$ characters from $1$ to $9$ without any whitespaces — the correct solution of the Sudoku puzzle.
output: For each test case, print the answer — the initial field with at most $9$ changed elements so that the obtained field is anti-sudoku. If there are several solutions, you can print any. It is guaranteed that the answer exists.
